reasoning: `va_1g_valid` has `#[verifier(when_used_as_spec(spec_va_2m_valid))]` — it should be `spec_va_1g_valid`. This copy-paste bug means in spec contexts `va_1g_valid(va)` resolves to `spec_va_2m_valid(va)` instead of `spec_va_1g_valid(va)`, silently weakening 1G address validation throughout `wf_mapping_1g` and any other spec usage.

This PHI merely constructs a zero PageEntry and confirms it satisfies `is_empty()` — a tautology about the definition of `is_empty()`. It does not actually demonstrate that `page_map_set` accepts problematic inputs; the `present == false ==> is_empty()` precondition is a deliberate design choice matching the `present_or_zero` invariant maintained across all page table levels.
